text.title: Software Development Best Practices 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the best-practices-2026 methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: tsconfig.json has strict: true AND noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true AND exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true.
rule.rationale: Strict mode catches the bug classes that consume the most LLM review time.

rule.statement: Python projects use ruff (lint + format) and mypy --strict (or pyright strict) in CI.
rule.rationale: Loose typing in 2026 is technical debt; CI without --strict ships untyped code that drifts.

rule.statement: Python deps managed via uv (or poetry). Raw pip install in CI is forbidden.
rule.rationale: uv pins reproducibly; raw pip lacks lockfile semantics by default.

rule.statement: Repository has .pre-commit-config.yaml with format + lint + typecheck hooks. Every commit runs them.
rule.rationale: Without pre-commit, CI catches issues late and roundtrips waste review time.

rule.statement: New Next.js projects start on the App Router (Next 13+) with React Server Components, not the Pages Router.
rule.rationale: App Router is the supported path forward; Pages Router is in maintenance mode.

rule.statement: Repo has an .aidocs/ structure with constitution.md + spec/design/test-plan per feature.
rule.rationale: Without an SDD scaffold, AI-assisted work drifts from explicit requirements.

rule.statement: If the stack is not TS or Python, or the repo has < 100 LOC, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Rubric is stack-specific; tiny prototypes do not benefit from baseline enforcement.
